scope: The npm distribution surface: what the published @spec-engine/spec-engine artifact contains (the Bun bundle, the guarded bin entry, the embedded docs payload) and the CLI surfaces that exist only because of it (spec docs, the non-Bun runtime guard). Belongs here: the tarball-content promises, the bin wrapper's runtime guard, and the offline docs command. Does not belong here: the webapp routes the artifact embeds (→ SERV), the compiled-binary channel (root build:cli, CI-owned), or the docs site's content itself (packages/site, not spec-governed). Boundary case: `spec docs` binds loopback-only like `spec serve`, but the promise lives here — it is a distribution surface over static files, not a view over the index.

requirements.statement: The spec docs command serves the prebuilt documentation site over HTTP bound to 127.0.0.1 only, and its --probe mode boots an ephemeral port, fetches /, and exits 0 only when the site title renders.
requirements.why: Docs that need the network die exactly when they are most needed (offline machines, sandboxed agents); a non-loopback bind would expose a local-only tool surface to the network.

requirements.statement: The spec docs command resolves its docs root relative to the running module (import.meta.url) — the npm payload dist/docs/ first, then the checkout build packages/site/dist — and a missing root exits 2 with guidance naming bun run build:site; the lookup is never cwd-relative.
requirements.why: A cwd-relative asset lookup breaks the moment the CLI runs from any directory but the package root — the classic embedded-UI bug; without guidance, a missing docs build reads as a broken install.

requirements.statement: The published bin entry checks for the Bun global before importing the engine bundle and, under any non-Bun runtime, exits 1 with an actionable install hint instead of a module-resolution crash.
requirements.why: The bundle statically imports bun:sqlite, which a Node ESM loader fails to resolve before any inline guard could run; engines.bun is advisory-only and postinstall checks are blocked by Bun's default trust policy, so the wrapper is the only enforcement point.

requirements.statement: The @spec-engine/spec-engine npm artifact ships only the built dist payload (bundle, bin wrapper, docs) plus manifest, README, and LICENSE, and its published dependencies never carry the workspace: protocol.
requirements.why: A tarball with raw source and tests bloats installs and leaks internals, and a single workspace:* string in published dependencies makes the package uninstallable for every consumer.
